sevenDayForecast.headline: Tropical low (30U) is expected to develop slowly in the Gulf of Carpentaria during the weekend.
points: Forecast Track Maps are being issued for 30U and should be referred to for more detailed information.
Tropical low 30U is forecast to move to the southeast of Saturday then turn to the west on Sunday.
It has a Moderate chance of developing into a tropical cyclone on the weekend and Monday before the risk reduces to Low from Tuesday onwards.
From Monday, 30U will continue to move west northwest, just off the northern Top End coast, across the Arafura Sea.

trackForecast.headline: Tropical Low 30U increasing risk of developing later Saturday and Sunday in Gulf of Carpentaria but then lower risk next week as it moves off NT coast.
trackForecast.discussion:
Satellite imagery has shown gradual development of 30U as it moves southeast across the northern Gulf of Carpentaria. 

Tropical low 30U is forecast to be slow moving on Saturday then turning to the west away from the Queensland coast.  

There are mixed signals for development and latest guidance does not suggest significant intensification. However, being in a low-shear environment means that an increase in deep convection can easily enhance gales near the centre especially south and west of the centre. 

The risk of 30U becoming a tropical cyclone increases to High (55%) for a period from late Saturday and early Sunday. The risk is Moderate to early Monday when it moves west northwest towards the Northern Territory coast. It is forecast to eventually move into the Timor Sea next week where the TC rating is low but this potential will be monitored in coming days.
